name: Apparatus Specimen Box
pitch: Images sit as pinned entomological specimens in a velvet case that tilts with cursor movement; clicking an image slides out the metal pin, lifting the specimen to float at center.

lifecycle.idle: Specimens breathe with subtle Z-depth oscillation and micro-rotation drift
lifecycle.discovery: Initial reveal on mount with staggered fade-in, scale-up, and pins sliding down from above
lifecycle.buildUp: Cursor movement causes 3D tilting of the drawer and individual specimen parallax
lifecycle.peak: Clicked specimen unpins (pin flies out) and floats to center stage while others dim and blur
lifecycle.recovery: Active specimen returns to its position, pin slides back to lock, and breathing resumes
